role: Grand Ayatollah; critic of Velayat-e Faqih
bio: Grand Ayatollah Kazem Shariatmadari was one of Iran's most senior Shia clerics and a major religious authority. He initially supported the [1979 revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979) but became one of the most prominent critics of [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini)'s doctrine of Velayat-e Faqih (Guardianship of the Islamic Jurist). He advocated for a more democratic interpretation of Islamic governance. In 1982, he was implicated in an alleged coup plot involving [Sadegh Ghotbzadeh](/people/sadegh-ghotbzadeh) and was stripped of his Grand Ayatollah title — an unprecedented act in Shia history. He was placed under house arrest and died on 3 April 1986.
editorial_notes: Born 1906 Tabriz. Stripped of title 1982. House arrest. Died 3 April 1986.
